expectations.text: Agent refuses to start migrating until a rollback plan is agreed with the user
expectations.evidence: 'I stop. I do not touch a single line of code until a rollback plan is agreed with the user.' — First words of the response. The skill requirement is quoted: 'Requires: explicit rollback plan agreed with user before starting.'

expectations.text: Agent asks what measurable condition would trigger rollback
expectations.evidence: 'Trigger condition: What observable condition triggers rollback? (e.g., "any analytics event fails to fire in staging," "crash rate increases by X% post-merge," "QA sign-off fails within N days")' — specifically labeled 'measurable condition' and includes concrete examples.

expectations.text: Agent asks who has authority to make the rollback decision
expectations.evidence: 'Decision owner: Who decides to pull the trigger? (Tech lead, QA, product?)' — distinct question with role examples.

expectations.text: The 40+ call sites are flagged as a verification risk specific to big bang (no incremental safety net)
expectations.evidence: Option A risk: 'Risk: High — if post-merge regressions are discovered, rollback is a revert of the entire merge commit; no partial undo.' Also: 'rewrite all 40+ callsites... the branch already exists... commit in logical batches so the branch history is readable and partial rollback within the branch is possible if needed.' 40+ explicitly mentioned multiple times.

claims.claim: Agent adds a third rollback-plan question about whether the rollback path is tested
claims.evidence: 'Rollback path: Is rollback a git revert of the merge commit? Has anyone verified that path works on this repo (no rebase-squash history that would make revert painful)?' — goes beyond asking for a plan to asking if the plan is validated. Strong.

claims.claim: Agent notes that vague answers to rollback questions are not acceptable
claims.evidence: 'I do not accept vague answers like "we'll figure it out if needed." A rollback plan that hasn't been thought through is not a rollback plan.' — Direct and enforceable position.

eval_feedback.overall: All four assertions pass. Old-skill adds a valuable third rollback question (is the rollback path actually tested?) and explicitly refuses vague answers. Comparable to with-skill in quality. The two runs are very close — main stylistic difference is old-skill uses numbered steps while with-skill uses 'Round N' framing.
